=
<= /div>

I want to start a new busin= ess. A dog walking business. I need your help to, one, build a website. Two= , think of ways to network and market that. And three, write me some introd= uctions that I want to send. For example, going around neighborhood areas w= here people buy and sell things. So I'm looking to create this whole thing.= I want to do this, by the way. I'm not going to advertise this as a cash b= usiness where people would just pay cash or they can hand me via Zelle, tha= t type of vehicle because it's kind of illegal, like not officially. But I = want to market the heck out of it. I also want you to give me a list of all= different other places in dog-related areas in, say within 45 minutes of w= here I live in Brookfield. So things like Newtown, Connecticut, New Milford= , obviously Brookfield, veterinarian places, dog grooming places, etc. wher= e I could go and network. With him help advertise myself in any other way t= hat I have a business. I quickly launched this whole new side gig business.=

Yes, let's build this properly= and launch your legitimate dog walking business fast. You'll stan= d out with professionalism, insurance, clear communication, and local relat= ionships =E2=80=94 exactly what pet owners want over gig apps or under-the-= table options.

I'll cover everyth= ing you asked for in order: quick legitimate setup, a ready-to-use professi= onal website, networking/marketing plan with a targeted local list, and rea= dy-to-send introductions/scripts.

1. Quick Legitimate Business Setup (Do This First =E2=80=94 1-3 Days)=

To operate officia= lly and market confidently:

  • Business= name ideas (pick one and check availability): Brookfield Paws Dog= Walking, Reliable Tails CT, Loyal Paws Professional Dog Walking, Happy Ste= ps Dog Walking Brookfield, or Tailored Walks CT. If using a name other than your legal name, file a simple DBA/Trade Name wi= th the Brookfield Town Clerk (inexpensive, quick).
  • Legal ba= sics: Start as sole proprietor. Get a free EIN from IRS.gov if you= want. Track every mile and expense (use Stride or MileIQ app =E2=80=94 mil= eage is a big deduction). Report income properly.
  • Insuranc= e (non-negotiable before your first paid walk): Get pet care/sitte= r liability insurance ($1M+ coverage). Good options include Pet Sitters Ass= ociates (PSA) or search =E2=80=9Cpet sitting insurance Connecticut.=E2=80= =9D Expect $150=E2=80=93400/year. This lets you honestly say =E2=80=9Cfully= insured=E2=80=9D and protects you. Many owners ask for proof.
  • Payments= & records (official, not cash-only): Use Square (free app + cheap card reader) or Wave (free invoicing) + Stripe/PayPal. Send pro= fessional invoices even if clients pay via Zelle or card. This creates a cl= ean paper trail. Never advertise =E2=80=9Ccash only=E2=80=9D or =E2=80=9CZe= lle/Venmo under the table.=E2=80=9D
  • Other qu= ick wins: Separate Google Voice number or business line. Simple cl= ient contract/intake form (I can draft one). Pet first-aid basics (short on= line cert helps credibility).

This setup lets yo= u market aggressively and legally while building trust fast.

2. Ready-to-Use Professional Website (Copy-Paste & Launch Today)<= /h3>

Here=E2=80=99s a c= omplete, beautiful, mobile-friendly single-page website built with Tailwind= CSS (no coding skills needed). It looks premium and trustworthy.

Instructions to launch in <1 hour:

  1. Copy the entire code block below into a = new file called index.h= tml on your computer.
  2. Open it in any browser to preview.
  3. Replace placeholders: [Your Business Name], [Your Full Name], phone, em= ail, specific prices if you change them, service areas, and photos.
  4. For booking: Create a free Calendly or Tid= yCal account (set your availability, e.g., Mon=E2=80=93Fri 9am=E2= =80=933pm), then replace the button/link with your scheduling link. Or use = a free form from Tally.so or Formspree (embeds easily).
  5. Host for= free instantly: Go to netlify.com =E2=86=92 drag & drop your = index.html file = (and any photos folder). You=E2=80=99ll get a free URL like yourname.netlify.app immediatel= y. Later buy a cheap domain (e.g., brookfieldpaws.com) for ~$10=E2=80=9315/= year and connect it.
  6. Add your own photos: Use your phone shot= s of happy dogs (get permission) or free stock from Unsplash.com (search = =E2=80=9Cprofessional dog walking=E2=80=9D or =E2=80=9Chappy dog on leash s= uburban=E2=80=9D). Replace the placeholder image comments.

    <!-- About -->
    <section class=3D"=
max-w-5xl mx-auto px-6 py-16"        <<=
/span>div class=3D"=
grid md:grid-cols-2 gap-12 items-center">
            &=
lt;div>
             =
   <h2 class=3D"text-4xl font-bold tracking-tight heading mb-6">Local care your dog will love</=
h2>
             =
   <div class=3D"space-y-4 text-lg text-stone-600">
             =
       <p>I'm a Brookfield resident and dedicated=
 dog lover offering professional, reliable dog walking services. Every walk=
 is tailored to your dog's personality, energy level, and needs.</p>
             =
       <p>I provide safe routes, plenty of water =
breaks (especially important in summer), and detailed updates with photos s=
o you always know your pup is happy and well cared for.</p>

font-medium">Background-=
checked &amp; insured</div&=
gt;

             =

             =
       <li class=3D"flex gap-x-3">=
;<i class=3D"fa-solid fa-check text-emerald-600 mt-1"></i<=
/span>> <span>Small groups only (max 4 dogs) for safety and attention</span></=
li>
             =
       <li class=3D"flex gap-x-3">=
;<i class=3D"fa-solid fa-check text-emerald-600 mt-1"></i<=
/span>> <span>Same walker every time =E2=80=94 consistency matters</<=
span style=3D"color: rgb(4, 68, 172);">span></li<=
/span>>

;<i class=3D"fa-solid fa-check text-emerald-600 mt-1"></i<=

             =
       <li class=3D"flex gap-x-3">=
;<i class=3D"fa-solid fa-check text-emerald-600 mt-1"></i<=
/span>> <span>Direct communication =E2=80=94 no app middleman</span></li>
             =
   </ul>
            &=
lt;/div>

div>

    <section id=3D"ser=
vices"=

        <<=
/span>div class=3D"=
max-w-6xl mx-auto px-6">=

            &=
lt;div class=3D"text-center mb-12"><=

lt;/h2>
             =

span style=3D"color: rgb(4, 68, 172);">p>
            &=
lt;/div>
            <=
/span>
            &=
lt;div class=3D"grid md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-6">
                <!-- Service cards -->
             =

 transition-all">=

             =

             =

             =
       <p class=3D"text-stone-600 text-sm mb-4">Perfect midday exercise or potty break</p>
             =
       <div class=3D"text-4xl font-bold tracking-tighter mb-1">$28</div>
             =
       <p class=3D"text-xs text-stone-500">per walk =E2=80=A2 individual or small group</p>

   </div>

             =

 transition-all">=

             =

>
             =
       <div class=3D"text-4xl font-bold tracking-tighter mb-1">$45</div>
             =
       <p class=3D"text-xs text-stone-500">per walk</p>
             =
   </div>
             =
   
             =
   <div class=3D"bg-stone-50 border border-stone-200 rounded-3xl p-7 hover:shadow-md=
 transition-all">=

             =
       <div class=3D"text-emerald-700 mb-4"><i class=3D"fa-solid fa-users text-3xl"></i></div>
             =
       <h3 class=3D"font-semibold text-xl mb-1">Group Walk (2=E2=80=934 dogs)</h3>=

             =
       <p class=3D"text-stone-600 text-sm mb-4">Socialization + exercise (same household or compatible dogs)=
</p>
             =
       <div class=3D"text-4xl font-bold tracking-tighter mb-1">$22</div>
             =
       <p class=3D"text-xs text-stone-500">per dog</p>
             =
   </div>
             =
   
             =
   <div class=3D"bg-emerald-700 text-white rounded-3xl p-7 flex flex-col">
             =
       <div class=3D"mb-auto">
             =
           <div class=3D"text-emerald-200 mb-4"><i<=
span style=3D"color: rgb(223, 134, 24); font-style: italic;"> class<=
span style=3D"color: rgb(0, 113, 206);">=3D"fa-solid fa-calendar-check text-3xl"></i></div>
             =

             =

             =
       </div>
             =
       <div>
             =
           <div class=3D"text-4xl font-bold tracking-tighter mb-1">$125</div>
             =

             =

             =
   </div>

lt;/div>

lt;p class=3D"text-center text-sm text-stone-500 mt-8">Custom packages, vacation care (multiple daily visits), =

as" cl=
ass=3D"max-w-5xl mx-auto px-6 py-16">

mary area</span></div><=

xt-center text-xs text-stone-500 mt-6">Routes chosen for safety, shade, and interest. We avoid busy=

    <section id=3D"why=
" clas=
s=3D"bg-white py-16 border-y border-stone-200"=
>
        <<=
/span>div class=3D"=
max-w-5xl mx-auto px-6">=

            &=
lt;div class=3D"text-center mb-10"><=
/span>
             =
   <h2 class=3D"text-4xl font-bold tracking-tight heading">Why local pet owners choose us</h2>
            &=
lt;/div>
            &=
lt;div class=3D"grid md:grid-cols-3 gap-8">
             =
   <div class=3D"text-center">
             =
       <div class=3D"mx-auto w-14 h-14 bg-emerald-100 rounded-full flex items-center=

t;i class=3D=

             =

mp; Professional</h3>
             =

             =
   </div>
             =
   <div class=3D"text-center">
             =

t;i class=3D=

             =

             =

how your dog did.</p>
             =
   </div>
             =
   <div class=3D"text-center">
             =
       <div class=3D"mx-auto w-14 h-14 bg-emerald-100 rounded-full flex items-center=

t;i class=3D=

></div>
             =

             =
       <p class=3D"text-stone-600 text-sm">I treat every dog like my own =E2=80=94 with patience, respect, a=
nd attention to their individual needs.</p>
             =
   </div>

lt;/div>
        </=
div>

    <!-- Testimonials (replace with real ones quickly) -->
    <section class=3D"=
max-w-5xl mx-auto px-6 py-16"        <<=
/span>h2 class=3D"t=
ext-center text-3xl font-bold tracking-tight heading mb-10">What clients are saying</h2>
        <<=
/span>div class=3D"=
grid md:grid-cols-3 gap-6"&=
gt;
            &=

Pro tip: Once live, create a free Google Business Profile (search =E2=80=9CGoogle Busi= ness Profile dog walker=E2=80=9D) =E2=80=94 this is huge for local =E2=80= =9Cdog walker near me=E2=80=9D searches and Maps visibility. Add photos and= your website link.

3. Networking & Marketing Plan + Local Dog-Related Places List

High-impact, low-cost plan for quick clients:

Priority actions this week:

  • Create Google Business Profile + Nextdoo= r posts.
  • Visit 6=E2=80=9310 local businesses in p= erson with nice business cards/flyers (Canva has great free =E2=80=9Cdog wa= lking=E2=80=9D templates =E2=80=94 add QR code to your site).
  • Post on Nextdoor and local FB groups.
  • Get 3=E2=80=935 early clients (offer lau= nch discount) =E2=86=92 ask for reviews/testimonials.

Targeted list of places within ~45 min of Brookfield (focus o= n these first =E2=80=94 all dog-owner heavy traffic):

Brookfield (start here =E2=80=94 everything is local):

  • Brookfie= ld Animal Hospital (Vet) =E2=80=94 329 Federal Rd, (203) 775-3679.= High-traffic waiting room.
  • Marta=E2= =80=99s Vineyard Canine Resort (Boarding/Daycare) =E2=80=94 519 Fe= deral Rd, (203) 775-4404. Perfect =E2=80=94 clients away need walks.
  • Petco Br= ookfield (Supplies + Grooming) =E2=80=94 169 Federal Rd, (203) 775= -3337.
  • Paws n= =E2=80=99 Relax CT (Grooming) =E2=80=94 Brookfield (Facebook for d= etails). Cage-free, relaxed vibe.
  • Magic To= uch Cat & Dog Grooming =E2=80=94 640 Federal Rd area.
  • PawNatur= al Pet Foods / Thomaston Feed (Specialty/natural foods) =E2=80=94 = Brookfield, (203) 546-7722. Quality-focused clients.

Danbury (~10 min):

  • Woof Gan= g Bakery & Grooming Danbury =E2=80=94 115 Mill Plain Rd, (203)= 404-0054. Grooming + retail.
  • PetSmart= Danbury =E2=80=94 ~57 North St area (grooming, training, events).=
  • Wags Dog= gie Daycamp =E2=80=94 47 Miry Brook Rd, (203) 744-9247 (also New M= ilford location).

Newtown (~10=E2=80=9315 min):

  • Cassio P= et Resort & Training Center =E2=80=94 Newtown, (203) 426-2881.= Upscale boarding/training.
  • Newtown = Park & Bark (Public off-leash dog park) =E2=80=94 23 Old Farm = Rd. Great for casual conversations with owners (soft approach only).

New Milford (~15=E2=80=9320 min):

  • A Muddy = Paw Pet Salon =E2=80=94 259 Danbury Rd, (860) 350-8991 / 3249.
  • New Milf= ord Animal Hospital (Vet).
  • Animal W= elfare Society (Shelter) =E2=80=94 8 Dodd Rd, (860) 354-1350. New = adopters often need walking help.

Other good spots: Mobile groomers (Super Pets Groomi= ng, Lunar Dog Spa =E2=80=94 they cover the area and may refer), real estate= offices (new homeowners with dogs), and community boards at libraries/coff= ee shops if allowed.

How to approach (see scripts below): Be friendly, br= ief, and offer mutual value (=E2=80=9CI=E2=80=99ll refer clients needing gr= ooming/boarding/vet care to you=E2=80=9D).

Other strong marketing channels:

  • Nextdoor= (best for quick local clients) and targeted Facebook groups (=E2= =80=9CNew Milford Neighbors=E2=80=9D, =E2=80=9CBrookfield CT=E2=80=9D, pet-= related groups).
  • Instagram business page =E2=80=94 post d= aily (tips + cute walk moments with permission). Use Reels.
  • Annual events like Pets in the Park in N= ew Milford (great visibility).
  • Later: Small Google/Facebook ads targeti= ng local zips.

4. Ready-to-Use Introductions & Scripts

In-person / front desk script (for groomers, vets, b= oarding): =E2=80=9CHi, I=E2=80=99m [Your Name] and I=E2=80=99m launching a profession= al, fully insured dog walking service right here serving Brookfield, Newtow= n, and New Milford. I noticed you have a lot of wonderful dog clients. Many= owners need reliable midday walks or exercise while they=E2=80=99re at wor= k.

I=E2=80=99d love t= o be a helpful resource for your clients and can refer people needing groom= ing or boarding back to you. Would it be okay if I left a small stack of ca= rds or flyers? Happy to create custom referral cards for your team too.=E2= =80=9D

Nextdoor / Facebook group post (copy-paste and tweak= ): =E2=80=9CHi neighbors! =F0=9F=91=8B I=E2=80=99m excited to launch [Your Bus= iness Name] =E2=80=94 a fully insured, professional dog walking service bas= ed right here in Brookfield serving Newtown, New Milford, and nearby towns.=

I provide safe, pe= rsonalized walks (individual or small groups) with photo + text updates aft= er every walk so you always know your dog is happy and well cared for.

Launch special: First 30-minute walk 25% off for new= clients this month.

DM me, text [your = phone], or book directly at [your website or Calendly link]. References ava= ilable. Looking forward to helping your pups get great exercise! =F0=9F=90= =BE #BrookfieldCT #NewtownCT #DogWalking=E2=80=9D

Short email or DM version (to businesses): Subject: Local Dog Walking Partnership Idea for [Business Name]

Hi [Manager Name o= r =E2=80=9CTeam=E2=80=9D],

I=E2=80=99m launch= ing a professional dog walking service in Brookfield and the surrounding ar= ea. I=E2=80=99m reaching out because many of your clients could benefit fro= m reliable midday walks or exercise between grooming/boarding appointments.=

I=E2=80=99m fully = insured, local, and committed to excellent communication with owners. I=E2= =80=99d be happy to refer clients needing your services and would love to l= eave some information for your clients or explore a simple referral partner= ship.

Would you be open = to a quick chat or me dropping off materials? Thank you for the great work = you do for local pets.

Best regards, [Your Name] [Your Business Name] | [Phone] | [Website]

You now have every= thing to launch this week legitimately and market it strongly. Start with t= he website + Google Business Profile + 4=E2=80=935 in-person visits + Nextd= oor post. You=E2=80=99ll get momentum fast.

Want me to:

  • Customize the website code further?
  • Draft a client contract/intake form?
  • Create Canva flyer/business card text or= Instagram captions?
  • Refine pricing or packages?
  • Help with anything else (insurance resea= rch, more scripts, etc.)?

Just say the word = and we=E2=80=99ll keep building. You=E2=80=99ve got this =E2=80=94 professi= onal, local, and reliable will win in this market. =F0=9F=90=BE

=

AKLSO I WNAT TI MAKE AN AD= D O FLYER TO GIVE TO BUSIENSS I NETWORK WITH

=
<= div class=3D"relative group flex flex-col justify-center w-full max-w-[var(= --content-max-width)] pb-0.5 items-start" id=3D"response-38fe481d-1c33-40fe= -a1d4-d0b103e1eafb" style=3D"scroll-margin-top: var(--scroll-margin-top, 0p= x);">
=
=
Perfect =E2=80=94 let's create a professional flyer/ad you can = print and hand to businesses (vets, groomers, boarding facilities, pet stor= es). This will help with networking and mutual referrals.

Quick Recommendation for Design

Go to Canva.com (free) and search for "dog walker flyer" or "pet sitting flyer". They have dozens of ready-made, custom= izable templates (many printable for cheap at Staples or Vistaprint).

etsy.com
brandpacks.com
brandpacks.com
=

Choose a clean, mo= dern design with paw prints, happy dogs, greens/blues/oranges for trust and= energy. Size: 8.5x11" (flyer) or 4x6"/business card size for easy handing.= Add your photo (you with a dog) or a nice stock dog image. Include a QR code linking to your website or Cale= ndly (free QR generator in Canva).

Suggested Flyer Content (Copy-Paste into Canva)

Headline (big, bold at top): Professional Dog Walking Services Reliable =E2=80=A2 Insured =E2=80=A2 Local = Brookfield Area

Sub-headline: Safe, personalized walks with photo updates after every walk. Your dog gets= the exercise and joy they deserve while you're at work or away.

Your Business Info (prominent): [Your Business Name] [Your Full Name], Owner =F0=9F=93=9E [Your Phone Number] | =F0=9F=93=A7 [Your Email] =F0=9F=8C=90 [Your Website or Calendly Link] Serving Brookfield, Newtown, New Milford & nearby towns

Key Benefits (bullets, with icons if possible):

  • Fully insured for your peace of mind
  • Photo & text updates after every wal= k
  • Flexible scheduling (midday, evenings, p= ackages)
  • Individual or small group walks (max 4 d= ogs for safety & attention)
  • Punctual, experienced, and passionate ab= out dogs
  • First walk special / referral program av= ailable

Services & Pricing (simple table or bullets):

  • 30-Min Walk: $28
  • 60-Min Adventure: $45
  • Group Walk: $22 per dog
  • Weekly packages available (save 10=E2=80= =9315%)

Call to Action (bottom, bold): Great for your clients! Refer dog owners needing daily exercise or midday breaks. I'll refer client= s needing grooming, boarding, or vet care back to you. Mutual partnership welcome =E2=80=94 let's help local pets together! Contact me to leave cards/flyers or discuss referrals.

Footer (small text): Fully insured professional service. Background-checked. References availabl= e. [Your Business Name] =E2=80=94 Proudly serving the Greater Brookfield area.=

Tips for Effective Flyers

  • Colors: Earthy greens, blues, warm oranges =E2=80=94 trustworthy and fun.<= /li>
  • Fonts: Clean sans-serif for body, bold playful for headline.
  • Images: One large happy dog(s) on leash + paw prints. Avoid clutter.
  • Print: 50=E2=80=93100 copies on nice cardstock (matte or glossy). Cost ~$2= 0=E2=80=9350 at Staples.
  • QR Code<= /strong>: Link to your website booking page or a short form.
  • Version = for businesses: Add a line like "P= artner Referral Program =E2=80=94 Ask me for custom cards you can = give your clients!"

Networking Handout Script (when giving the flyer)

"Hi [Name], thanks= for taking a minute. Here's a flyer for my new professional dog walking se= rvice. Many of your clients could use reliable midday walks. I'm happy to r= efer people to you for [grooming/boarding/vet services] and would love to b= e a resource for yours. Feel free to keep these cards or let me know if a p= artnership makes sense!"

This flyer positio= ns you as professional and collaborative =E2=80=94 exactly what businesses = like.

If you want me to:=

  • Refine the text (e.g., specific pricing = or name)
  • Draft a business card version (smaller)<= /li>
  • Help with Canva-specific instructions or= more images
  • Generate a custom flyer image (describe = changes if you have a base)

Just paste your bu= siness name, exact details, or a rough draft, and I'll iterate. We can have= this ready to print quickly! =F0=9F=90=BE

(You can also hand= smaller business cards with the same core info for easier distribution.)

Bethel, CT:

I NEED A BIZ NAME

Here ar= e strong, memorable business name ideas tailored for your dog walk= ing side gig in Brookfield CT (and surrounding towns). I prioritized:

  • Local feel (Brookfield/Newtown area)
  • Dog/Corgi theme (using your two adorable= Corgis in the logo/marketing)
  • Professional yet approachable (trust-bui= lding for pet owners)
  • Easy to brand (website, flyers, Google B= usiness, Instagram)

Top Recommendations (My Favorites)

  1. Corgi Cr= ew Dog Walking =E2=80=94 Perfect! Highlights your two Corgis as th= e "crew." Fun, unique, and memorable. Logo with your dogs looks amazing.
  2. Twin Tai= ls Dog Walking =E2=80=94 References your pair of Corgis. Short, ca= tchy, and cute.
  3. Brookfie= ld Paws Dog Walking =E2=80=94 Strong local anchor + professional. = Easy for "near me" searches.
  4. Reliable= Corgi Walks =E2=80=94 Emphasizes reliability (key for pet service= s) and your dogs.

Other Great Options

  • Happy Co= rgi Steps / Corgi Steps Dog Walkin= g
  • Palicz P= aws Professional Dog Walking (personal touch)
  • Loyal Ta= ils Brookfield
  • Paw Patr= ol CT (playful, but check trademark)
  • Golden &= amp; Black Paws (nod to your dogs' colors)
  • Neighbor= hood Tail Waggers
  • Safe Paw= s Dog Walking Brookfield

Quick Tips for Choosing & Using

  • Check av= ailability: Google the name + "CT" for competitors. Check CT Secre= tary of State for business name (or just file DBA at town clerk). Domain li= ke corgicrew.com or brookfieldpaws.com (~$10=E2=80=9315/yr on Namecheap).
  • Logo rea= dy: Your Corgis photo already works great with Corgi Crew or Twin Ta= ils. I can generate more logo versions with your chosen name.
  • Marketin= g fit: Use in your website (I can update the HTML code instantly),= flyer ("Proudly owned by two Corgis!"), Google Business, and Nextdoor post= s.
  • Tagline = ideas (pair with any name): "Local =E2=80=A2 Insured =E2=80=A2 Cor= gi-Approved Walks" or "Personalized walks with photo updates from a fellow = dog parent."

Which one(s) do you like best? Or give me preference= s (e.g., must include "Corgi", "Brookfield", your last name, fun vs profess= ional) and I=E2=80=99ll generate more + updated logo/flyer/website with the= name locked in.

We can finalize th= is today and move to flyers, website tweaks, or first client scripts! =F0= =9F=90=BE

Revised Flyer Content (with New Name)

Headline: Corgi Crew Dog Walking Professional =E2=80=A2 Insured =E2=80=A2 Fun Walks for Your Dog

Sub-headline: Proudly featuring our own two Corgis =E2=80=94 we know what happy, healthy = walks look like! Safe, personalized service with photo updates.

Okay, two things. One, what do you think the total startup costs ar= e going to be? I want to do this as cheap as possible. And number two, give= me everything I need to do in the exact order, step by step, as if I'm a 1= 0-year-old.

=

=E2=9C=85 Here=E2= =80=99s everything you asked for, super clear and simple.

1. Total Startup Costs (as cheap as possible)

You can start this= business legitimately and safely for under= $400 total if you do it smart and use free stuff where possible. = Here=E2=80=99s the realistic cheap breakdown:

=
= = =
I= temCheap CostNotes (how to keep it l= ow)
Busine= ss Name Registration (DBA / Trade Name)$20Go to Brookfield Town Clerk. Super easy and req= uired if using "Corgi Crew".
Insurance (most important!)$190=E2=80=93$250 for first yearGet Pet Care Insurance (PCI)= =E2=80=94 starts around $15=E2=80=9318 per month. This protects you if any= thing happens with a dog. Do not s= kip this.
Logo$0= We already made it with = your Corgis! Use the ones I generated.
Website$0Use free Netlify or Carrd free plan + the HTML I gave you.
Flyers + Business Cards$3= 0=E2=80=93$50Print 100 f= lyers + 100 cards at Staples or home printer on nice paper.
Free Phone Number$0Google Voice (free business number th= at forwards to your phone).
Basic Supplies (poop bags, treat pouch, water bottle, leash if = needed)$20=E2=80=93$40Buy= cheap on Amazon or use what you already have at home.
= Domain name (optional at first)$0= =E2=80=93$12Skip for now= or grab corgicrew.com / corgicrewct.com later.
Google Business Profile$0Free listing so people find you on Google M= aps.
<= div class=3D"[&>svg_*]:stroke-current" style=3D"width: 16px; height:= 16px;">

Total Cheap Startup Cost: $260 =E2=80=93 $370

This gets you full= y legal, insured, with branding and marketing materials. You can start even= cheaper (~$220) by skipping printing at first and doing everything digital= + in-person chats, but having nice flyers/cards makes networking way easie= r when you visit vets and groomers.

2. Step-by-Step Plan (Explained Like You=E2=80=99re 10 Years Old)

Imagine I=E2=80=99= m your big helper and we=E2=80=99re doing this together like a fun project.= Do the steps in this exact order:=

  1. Love you= r business name We already picked Corgi Crew Dog Walking because it=E2=80=99s fun and uses your two awesome Corgis! Say it ou= t loud a few times. That=E2=80=99s your team name now.
  2. Make you= r cool logo We already created logos with pictures of your two Corgis! Download the one= s I made. They look professional and cute. Use them everywhere.
  3. Get insu= rance first (super important safety rule) Buy pet-walking insurance before you walk anyone else=E2=80=99s dog. Go to = petcareins.com and get a quote for =E2=80=9Cdog walker=E2=80=9D. It costs a= bout $15=E2=80=9320 a month and protects you if a dog gets hurt or somethin= g happens. This is the #1 thing that makes you look like a real pro.
  4. Register= your business name with the town Go to the Brookfield Town Clerk=E2=80=99s office (or their website). Fill o= ut the simple =E2=80=9CTrade Name=E2=80=9D form for =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog= Walking=E2=80=9D. Pay $20. They=E2=80=99ll give you a paper that says it= =E2=80=99s official. Takes 15=E2=80=9330 minutes.
  5. Get a fr= ee business phone number Go to voice.google.com on your computer. Make a free Google Voice number (l= ike a second phone number just for the business). It rings on your real pho= ne. Write it on everything.
  6. Make you= r free website Copy the full HTML code I gave you earlier into a file called index.html. Open it in your= browser to see your beautiful website. Then go to netlify.com, drag the fi= le in, and it goes live for free with a web address. (We can add your logo = and name right now if you want.)
  7. Make fly= ers and business cards Go to Canva.com (free). Search =E2=80=9Cdog walker flyer=E2=80=9D. Pick a n= ice template. Put your Corgi Crew logo, phone number, prices, and =E2=80=9C= First walk special!=E2=80=9D on it. Print 100 flyers and 100 small cards ch= eap at Staples. These are what you hand to vets and groomers.
  8. Tell Goo= gle you exist (free advertising) Go to business.google.com. Make a free listing for =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog = Walking=E2=80=9D. Add your logo, phone, service area (Brookfield + nearby t= owns), and photos of your dogs. This helps people find you when they search= =E2=80=9Cdog walker near me=E2=80=9D.
  9. Tell the= neighborhood and dog places about you
    • Post on Nextdoor and local Facebook grou= ps: =E2=80=9CHi! I=E2=80=99m starting Corgi Crew Dog Walking with my two Co= rgis=E2=80=A6=E2=80=9D
    • Print your flyers and go visit vets, gro= omers, and pet stores in Brookfield, Bethel, Danbury, Newtown, etc. Smile, = be nice, and say =E2=80=9CI=E2=80=99d love to help your clients who need wa= lks!=E2=80=9D
  10. Start wa= lking dogs and have fun! Once you have insurance and the basics, offer your first walks (maybe to fr= iends/family with dogs first for practice and reviews). Keep it simple: be = on time, send cute photos after walks, and charge your prices. Word-of-mout= h will bring more clients fast.

Bonus kid-friendly tips:

  • Always walk safe routes.
  • Never leave dogs alone.
  • Take pictures and send them to owners so= they feel happy.
  • Keep track of money in a notebook or fre= e app.

You now have the <= strong class=3D"font-semibold">cheapest safe way to start and the = exact order to do everything witho= ut getting overwhelmed.

ALOS ANYTHING E= LS EI CNA DO MYSLEF TO REDCUE COSTS?

Yes! Here are extra ways you c= an do things yourself to cut costs even more (keeping everything s= afe and legitimate). These are easy things you can handle personally:

Super Cheap / Free DIY Ideas (Save Another $100+)

  • Print yo= ur own flyers & cards =E2=86=92 Use Canva (free) on your compu= ter. Design them, then print at home on regular paper (or nicer paper from = Walmart). Or take the file to a library printer (very cheap, like 10 cents = per page). Skip Staples completely.
  • Use free= tools only:
    • Google Docs or Canva free version for al= l documents (contracts, intake forms, flyers).
    • Free Google Voice for your business phon= e.
    • Free Netlify or GitHub Pages to host you= r website forever (no monthly fee).
    • Free Google Business Profile (already in= cluded).
  • Supplies= from home or dollar store:
    • Poop bags: Buy a big roll at Dollar Tree= or reuse grocery bags at first.
    • Treats & water bottle: Use what you = already have for your Corgis.
    • Leashes/harnesses: Start with what you o= wn. Only buy extras if a client needs a specific size.
    • No fancy uniform needed =E2=80=94 wear a= nice t-shirt with your logo printed at home (iron-on transfer from Amazon = for $5=E2=80=9310).
  • Marketin= g for free:
    • Post on Nextdoor, Facebook groups, and l= ocal community pages yourself (no paid ads yet).
    • Ask friends/family with dogs for the fir= st few walks at a discount or free in exchange for testimonials and photos.=
    • Hand-write thank-you notes or simple inf= o sheets instead of fancy printing.
  • Skip the= se for now:
    • Fancy domain name (use the free Netlify = link like corgicrew.netlify.app).
    • Business cards (use flyers cut in half o= r just your phone number written nicely).
    • Software/apps (use free Google Calendar = and a notebook for scheduling and tracking money).
  • Insuranc= e tip: Shop quotes yourself on petcareins.com or similar sites. St= art with the cheapest monthly plan that still covers liability. Pay month-t= o-month at first instead of yearly.

With all these DIY tricks, you can realistically get started = for $200=E2=80=93$300 total (mostly insurance + $20 registration += a few supplies).

You=E2=80=99re alr= eady doing the smartest thing by asking questions and using free tools like= the website HTML and logos I made for you.

Anything Else?

  • Track every penny you spend in a noteboo= k or free Google Sheet (this helps at tax time too).
  • Start small =E2=80=94 only take 3=E2=80= =935 dogs at first so you don=E2=80=99t need lots of supplies right away.

If you want a super simple shopping list or free contract template I can write for you= , or help with anything specific (like a home-printed flyer design idea), j= ust tell me.

You=E2=80=99re doi= ng great keeping costs low while still running it the right way. What=E2=80= =99s the next thing you want to tackle? (Insurance quote? Website upload? F= lyer text?)

=
<= svg xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink=3D"http://www.w3.org/1= 999/xlink" viewBox=3D"0 0 24 24" width=3D"24" height=3D"24" preserveAspectR= atio=3D"xMidYMid meet" style=3D"width: 100%; height: 100%; transform: trans= late3d(0px, 0px, 0px); content-visibility: visible;"><= /g>

= CAN I MAY TIS MONTLY VS ALL AT ONCE: Insurance (most important!)$190=E2=80= =93$250 for first yearGet Pet Care Insurance (PCI) =E2=80=94 starts around = $15=E2=80=9318 per month. This protects you if anything happens with a dog.= Do not skip this.

Yes, you c= an (and probably should) pay monthly for insurance at the beginning.

Pet Care Insurance (PCI) and most pet sitter/dog wal= ker plans let you pay month-to-month (around $15=E2=80=93$18 per month). You don=E2=80=99t have to pay the full year upfront.

Quick Advice on This:

  • Start wi= th monthly =E2=80=94 This keeps your initial cash outlay super low= (just the first month ~$15=E2=80=9320 + the $20 DBA fee). You can always s= witch to yearly later if you want a small discount.
  • Still ge= t it immediately =E2=80=94 Insurance is the #1 safety thing. Get a= quote and buy the first month before you walk any client=E2=80=99s dog.
  • How to d= o it:
    1. Go to petcareins.com (or search =E2=80= =9CPet Care Insurance dog walker=E2=80=9D).
    2. Fill out the quick quote form for =E2=80= =9Cdog walking=E2=80=9D or =E2=80=9Cpet sitting=E2=80=9D.
    3. Choose the basic liability plan (that=E2= =80=99s what you need).
    4. Pick monthly payment.

This way your tota= l startup stays very low while you=E2=80=99re protected right away.

Reminder on the full cheap startup order (from befor= e):

  1. Name + logo (done)
  2. Insurance (do this next =E2=80=94 monthl= y)
  3. DBA registration ($20 at town clerk)
  4. Free website + Google Business
  5. Print cheap flyers yourself
  6. Start telling people

  • Give you the direct link or exact steps = for the insurance quote?
  • Update anything else (flyer, website, ch= ecklist)?

You=E2=80=99re mak= ing smart money choices =E2=80=94 monthly insurance is perfect for starting= small. Just don=E2=80=99t skip it! Let me know the next step. =F0=9F=90=BE=

= =E2=9C=85 Here=E2=80=99s your complete, updated, and simplified Business Pl= an for Corgi Crew Dog Walking.

I=E2=80=99ve combi= ned everything we=E2=80=99ve built together (name, logo with your two Corgi= s, cheap startup costs, monthly insurance, step-by-step launch plan, market= ing ideas, cost-saving tips, website, flyer, and more) into one clear docum= ent you can follow.

Corgi Crew Dog Walking

Business Plan =E2=80=93 Simple & Cheap Launch Version Owner: [Your Name] Location: Brookfield, CT + nearby = towns Date: June 2026

= 1. Business Overview (What You=E2=80=99re D= oing)

  • Business= Name: Corgi Crew Dog Walking
  • Tagline = ideas: =E2=80=9CHappy walks with happy dogs=E2=80=9D or =E2=80=9CC= orgi-approved walks you can trust=E2=80=9D
  • What you= offer: Professional, insured, reliable dog walking with photo + t= ext updates after every walk.
  • Why it= =E2=80=99s special: You=E2=80=99re a local dog parent with your ow= n two Corgis (featured in the logo). You treat every dog like family.
  • Goal: Start small as a side gig, stay legitimate and safe, keep costs very= low, and grow through word-of-mouth and local networking.

= 2. Branding (Already Done!)

  • Logo: We created multiple versions using photos of your two Corgis (orange= /white + black/white). Use the clean professional one for most things.
  • Website:= Ready-to-use single-page site with the name, services, and your d= ogs. Host it for free on Netlify.
  • Colors:<= /strong> Friendly greens + warm tones (trust + fun).

= 3. Services & Pricing (Start Simple)

  • 30-minute walk: $28
  • 60-minute adventure walk: $45
  • Small group walks (2=E2=80=934 dogs): $22 per dog
  • Weekly package (5=C3=97 30-min walks): <= strong class=3D"font-semibold">$125 (best value)

Launch special: First walk 20=E2=80=9325% off for ne= w clients.

= 4. Service Area

Brookfield + towns= within ~30=E2=80=9345 minutes: Newtown, Danbury, Bethel, New Milford, Ridg= efield, Southbury, Redding, etc.

= 5. Startup Costs =E2=80=93 Cheapest Legitim= ate Version

Total: $260 =E2=80=93 $370 (you can do it for ~$200= =E2=80=93$250 if you print at home and start super small)

=
I= temCostHow to Keep It Cheap
DBA / Trade Name registration$20Brookfiel= d Town Clerk
Insurance (first year)$190=E2=80=93$250Pay monthly (~$15=E2=80=9318/m= o) with Pet Care Insurance (PCI)
Logo$0Already m= ade with your Corgis
Website$0Free Netlify + H= TML code I gave you
Flyers + business cards$0=E2=80=93$50Design in free Canva, print at home or library
= Google Voice number$0Free business phone number
= Basic supplies (poop bags, treats, pouch, water bottle)$20=E2=80=93$40Use what you have + Dollar Tree/Amazon
TOTAL= $260=E2=80=93$370=E2=80=94

Money-saving tips you can do yourself:

  • Pay insurance monthly at first.
  • Print flyers/cards at home or library.
  • Skip fancy domain for now (use free Netl= ify link).
  • Start with friends/family dogs for pract= ice and reviews.
  • Use free tools only (Canva, Google Docs,= Google Calendar, notebook for tracking).

= 6. Exact Step-by-Step Launch Plan (Explaine= d Simply)

Do these in order,= like a checklist:

  1. Love you= r name & logo =E2=80=94 Done! =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog Walking= =E2=80=9D + your two Corgis in the logo.
  2. Get insu= rance first (most important safety step) =E2=80=94 Go to petcarein= s.com, get a quote for dog walking, choose monthly payment, buy it before w= alking any client dog.
  3. Register= your business name =E2=80=94 Go to Brookfield Town Clerk (or thei= r website). Fill out the Trade Name form and pay $20. Get your official pap= er.
  4. Set up f= ree business phone =E2=80=94 Create a free Google Voice number at = voice.google.com.
  5. Build &a= mp; launch your free website =E2=80=94 Copy the full HTML code I g= ave you, save as index.= html, drag it into netlify.com for a free live site.
  6. Make you= r marketing materials =E2=80=94 Use Canva (free) to create flyers = and business cards with your logo, phone, services, and launch special. Pri= nt 50=E2=80=93100 cheaply.
  7. Claim yo= ur free Google listing =E2=80=94 Go to business.google.com and cre= ate =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog Walking=E2=80=9D. Add logo, photos of your dogs= , service area, and phone.
  8. Start fr= ee online marketing =E2=80=94 Post on Nextdoor and local Facebook = groups. Be friendly and offer the launch special.
  9. Network = in person =E2=80=94 Print flyers. Visit vets, groomers, boarding p= laces, and pet stores in Brookfield, Bethel, Danbury, Newtown, etc. Smile, = introduce yourself, and offer to refer clients back to them.
  10. Start wa= lking dogs safely =E2=80=94 Begin with easy clients. Always be on = time, send photo updates, and keep great notes.

= 7. Marketing & Getting Clients=

  • Best fre= e methods: Nextdoor, local Facebook groups, Google Business Profil= e, and in-person visits to pet businesses.
  • Networki= ng script (simple version): =E2=80=9CHi! I=E2=80=99m starting Corg= i Crew Dog Walking with my own two Corgis. I=E2=80=99d love to help your cl= ients who need reliable walks. Happy to refer people back to you for groomi= ng/boarding too.=E2=80=9D
  • Later id= eas: Ask happy clients for reviews and photos. Offer referral bonu= ses.

= 8. Operations & Safety (Keep It Simple = & Safe)

  • Always get basic info from owners (vet, = emergency contact, any behavior notes).
  • Use proper leashes/harnesses.
  • Never leave dogs unattended.
  • Take photos after every walk and text th= em to owners.
  • Track your mileage (big tax deduction) i= n a notebook or free app.
  • Keep everything professional and kind.

= 9. Next 30 Days Goal

  • Get insurance + DBA done this week.
  • Website + Google listing live by end of = week.
  • First 3=E2=80=935 clients through networ= king and local posts.
  • Collect testimonials and photos for mark= eting.

You now have everything in one place =E2=80=94 name,= logo, website, flyer ideas, cheap costs, monthly insurance option, and a s= uper clear step-by-step plan.

This plan keeps ev= erything legitimate, safe, and as cheap as possible while giving you a real= professional business you can be proud of.

DID YOU REVEIW AND INCLDUEN 100% OF EVEYTHINGB IN THIS CHAT?

<= /div>

Here=E2=80=99s a q= uick confirmation of everything covered:

=E2=9C=85 HERE IS YOUR COMPLETE, FINAL BUSINESS PLAN<= /strong> Corgi Crew Dog Walking Bare Minimum Startup Version =E2=80=93 Copy= & Paste Ready

You can copy every= thing below into a Word doc, Google Doc, or Notes app. I made it super simp= le (like explaining to a 10-year-old), broken into clear sections with exact steps, bare minimum costs, and tim= e estimates for each part.

Total realistic time to do everything and launch: 7=E2=80=9314 days if you work on it = a little each day (1=E2=80=933 hours/day). You can go faster or slower.

SECTION 1: Business Overview (What Yo= u Are Doing)

Time to read & understand: 10 minutes

You are starting a= real, legal dog walking business called Co= rgi Crew Dog Walking. You will walk other people=E2=80=99s dogs safely, send them photos after ev= ery walk, and treat every dog like your own two Corgis. You will do everything the right way (insurance + official name) so you are= protected and look professional. You will start small and cheap as a side gig and grow with happy customers = telling friends.

Goal: Launch with almost no money, stay 100% legal a= nd safe, and get your first paying walks within 1=E2=80=932 weeks.

SECTION 2: Business Name & Brandi= ng (Already Mostly Done!)

Time to finish this section: 30=E2=80=9360 minutes

Business Name: Corgi Crew Dog Walking Why this name is perfect: It uses = your two Corgis, sounds fun, and works anywhere (no town name like =E2=80= =9CBrookfield=E2=80=9D so you can grow later).

Logo: You already have great logos with photos of yo= ur two Corgis (one orange/white, one black/white). Use the clean profession= al version for everything.

Tagline ideas (pick one):

  • =E2=80=9CHappy walks with happy dogs=E2= =80=9D
  • =E2=80=9CCorgi-approved walks you can tr= ust=E2=80=9D

What to do right now:

  1. Save the logo images I made for you.
  2. Decide on one tagline.

Total time for this section: ~1 hour max

SECTION 3: Services & Pricing (Ke= ep It Simple)

Time: 15 minutes

Services you will offer:

  • 30-minute walk
  • 60-minute adventure walk
  • Small group walks (2=E2=80=934 dogs)
  • Weekly package (5 walks) =E2=80=93 best = value

Bare minimum starting prices:

  • 30-minute walk =3D $28
  • 60-minute walk =3D $45
  • Group walk =3D $22 per dog
  • Weekly package (5 =C3=97 30-min) =3D $125 (saves customers money)

Launch special: First walk 20=E2=80=9325% off for ne= w clients.

Time for this section: 15 minutes

SECTION 4: Bare Minimum Startup Costs= (Cheapest Legal Way)

Time to read & plan: 10 minutes

Total cost to launch safely: $260 =E2=80=93 $370 (yo= u can do it for ~$200=E2=80=93$250 if you print at home)

Here is the exact = cheap list:

=
<= td class=3D"break-words ps-2.5 pe-2.5 py-2 leading-snug" data-col-size=3D"m= d" style=3D"white-space: pre-wrap;">DBA / Trade Name (official name registr= ation)=
I= temCostHow to pay the absolute minim= um
$20Go to Brookfield= Town Clerk =E2=80=93 pay cash or card
Insurance (most im= portant thing)$15=E2=80=93$18 per monthPay monthly with Pet= Care Insurance (PCI) =E2=80=93 do NOT pay yearly yet
Log= o= $0Already made with your= Corgis
Website$0Free on Netlify + the HTML code I gave you

$0Free at voice.google.com

TOTAL$260=E2=80=93$370Mostly insurance + $20 regis= tration
=

Money-saving rules you must follow:

  • Pay insurance monthly at the start.
  • Print everything yourself or at the libr= ary.
  • Use only free tools (Canva free, Google = Voice, Netlify free hosting).
  • Skip fancy domain name for now.
  • Start with friends/family dogs for pract= ice (cheaper and safer).

Time for this section: 10 minutes to read + 1 day to buy insu= rance & register name

SECTION 5: Step-by-Step Launch Plan (= Do These in Order =E2=80=93 Like a 10-Year-Old Checklist)

Total time for all steps: 7=E2=80=9314 days

Step 1: Get Insurance (Most Important =E2=80=93 Do This First= !) Time: 30=E2=80=9360 minutes today Go to petcareins.com =E2=86=92 get quote for =E2=80=9Cdog walker=E2=80=9D = =E2=86=92 choose monthly payment =E2=86=92 buy the first month right now. You must have this before walking any other dog.

Step 2: Register Your Business Name Time: 30=E2=80=9360 minutes (same day or next day) Go to Brookfield Town Clerk (in person or website). Fill out the simple Tra= de Name form for =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog Walking=E2=80=9D. Pay $20. Get you= r official paper.

Step 3: Get a Free Business Phone Number Time: 15 minutes Go to voice.google.com on your computer =E2=86=92 make a free Google Voice = number that rings on your real phone. Write this number on everything.

Step 4: Build & Launch Your Free Website Time: 1=E2=80=932 hours Copy the full HTML code I gave you earlier =E2=86=92 save as index.html =E2=86=92 go to net= lify.com =E2=86=92 drag the file in =E2=86=92 your site goes live for free.= Add your logo.

Step 5: Make Flyers & Business Cards Time: 1=E2=80=932 hours design + printing Go to Canva.com (free) =E2=86=92 search =E2=80=9Cdog walker flyer=E2=80=9D = =E2=86=92 use your logo, phone number, prices, and launch special. Print 50= =E2=80=93100 at home or library (super cheap).

Step 6: Set Up Free Google Listing Time: 30=E2=80=9360 minutes Go to business.google.com =E2=86=92 create =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog Walking= =E2=80=9D =E2=86=92 add logo, photos of your dogs, phone number, and servic= e area (Brookfield + nearby towns).

Step 7: Start Free Online Marketing Time: 30=E2=80=9360 minutes per day for first week Post on Nextdoor and local Facebook groups (Brookfield, Newtown, Bethel, Da= nbury, etc.). Be friendly and mention your launch special and Corgi Crew.

Step 8: Go Visit Pet Places in Person (Networking) Time: 2=E2=80=934 hours total (spread over a few days) Print flyers. Visit these places (start with closest):

  • Brookfield Animal Hospital
  • Marta=E2=80=99s Vineyard Canine Resort
  • Petco Brookfield
  • Paws n=E2=80=99 Relax CT
  • Woof Gang Danbury
  • Vets and groomers in Bethel, Newtown, Ne= w Milford, Southbury Smile, be nice, hand them a flyer, and offer to send customers to them too.=

Step 9: Start Walking Dogs Safely Time: Ongoing Begin with easy clients. Always be on time, send photo updates, and keep no= tes. Collect happy reviews and photos.

Total time for all 9 steps: 7=E2=80=9314 days (do 1= =E2=80=933 steps per day)

SECTION 6: Marketing Plan (How to Get= Customers Fast & Cheap)

Time to set up: 2=E2=80=933 hours total

Free & powerful methods (do these first):

  • Nextdoor posts
  • Local Facebook groups
  • Google Business Profile (people search = =E2=80=9Cdog walker near me=E2=80=9D)
  • In-person visits to vets/groomers (with = flyers)
  • Ask happy clients for reviews and photos=

Simple networking script: =E2=80=9CHi! I=E2=80=99m starting Corgi Crew Dog Walking with my own two Co= rgis. I=E2=80=99d love to help your clients who need reliable walks. Happy = to refer people back to you for grooming or boarding too!=E2=80=9D

SECTION 7: Daily Operations & Saf= ety (Keep It Easy & Safe)

Time to learn: 20 minutes

  • Get basic info from every owner (vet pho= ne, emergency contact, any special needs).
  • Use good leashes and harnesses.
  • Never leave dogs alone.
  • Take a photo after every walk and text i= t to the owner.
  • Track your miles in a notebook or free p= hone app (big tax help later).
  • Be on time and kind every single time.

SECTION 8: Full Timeline & Milest= ones

Total time to launch: 7=E2=80=9314 days

  • Day 1=E2= =80=932: Insurance + DBA registration + Google Voice
  • Day 2=E2= =80=933: Website live + Google Business Profile
  • Day 3=E2= =80=935: Flyers made + first online posts
  • Day 5=E2= =80=9310: Visit pet businesses + keep posting
  • Day 7=E2= =80=9314: First paying walks + collect reviews

By the end of 2 weeks you should have:

  • Insurance & official name
  • Website & Google listing live
  • Flyers ready
  • Some customers booked

You now have the complete bare-minimum business plan.

<= /div>
=

Yes =E2=80=94 you can launch for under $100 if you do= it exactly the way you described.

Bare-Minimum Realistic Breakdown (High-End but Still Cheap)

  • DBA / Tr= ade Name registration (Brookfield Town Clerk): $20 (required, one-time)
  • Insuranc= e (first month only, monthly plan): $8 (you found this rate =E2=80=94 great!)
  • Flyers &= amp; business cards: $0 (= you print them yourself at home or library)
  • Basic su= pplies (poop bags, treats, pouch, water bottle): $25
  • Website,= Google listing, Google Voice phone, logo: $0 (all free with what we already built)

Total to launch: ~$53 (well under $100)

You only pay the f= irst month of insurance right away. Everything else is free or already cove= red by things you have at home.

Important Honest Notes

  • Insurance is the biggest =E2=80=9Cmust-h= ave.=E2=80=9D Even at $8/month, make sure it actually covers liability for = dog walking (ask the company or read the policy). Do not skip it.
  • You can truly keep it under $100 if you = are strict about DIY (print at home, use what you already own for supplies)= .
  • Later months you=E2=80=99ll have the $8 = insurance + gas/mileage, but that comes from customer payments.

You are doing this= the smartest cheap way possible. Once you get a few regular clients, the b= usiness will quickly pay for itself and more.

SECTION 5: Step-by-Step Launch Plan (= Do These in Order)

Total time: 7=E2=80=9314 days

Step 1: Get Insurance First (Most Important =E2=80=93 Do This= Before Anything Else) Time: 30=E2=80=9360 minutes today or Monday You must have insurance before you= walk any other dog. Pay monthly.

Exact places to contact on Monday (copy these):

  1. Pet Care= Insurance (PCI) =E2=80=93 Best and easiest for beginners Website: petcareins.com Go to the site =E2=86=92 click =E2=80=9CGet Protected Today=E2=80=9D or =E2= =80=9CBuy My Policy=E2=80=9D =E2=86=92 get instant online quote for dog wal= king =E2=86=92 choose monthly payment. They start around $17.92/month. Very= popular for solo dog walkers.
  2. Pet Sitt= ers Associates (PSA) Website: petsitllc.com Click =E2=80=9CApply=E2=80=9D for membership + insurance. Basic plan around= $220/year (~$18/month). Good option.
  3. Business= Insurers of the Carolinas (good group rates) Phone: 1-800-962-4611 Or website: business-insurers.com or psi.petsitterinsurance.com Call and say you are a new solo dog walker in Connecticut. They work with p= et sitter associations for better rates.

Start with Pet Care Insurance (PCI) on Monday =E2=80= =94 it=E2=80=99s the fastest online.

Step 2: Register Your Business Name Time: 30=E2=80=9360 minutes (same day or next) Go to the Brookfield Town Clerk (in person or their website). Fill out the = simple =E2=80=9CTrade Name=E2=80=9D form for =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog Walkin= g=E2=80=9D. Pay the $20 fee. Get your official paper.

Step 3: Get a Free Business Phone Number Time: 15 minutes Go to voice.google.com =E2=86=92 make a free Google Voice number that rings= on your real phone. Use this number on everything.

Step 4: Launch Your Free Website Time: 1=E2=80=932 hours Copy the full HTML code I gave you earlier. Save it as a file called index.html. Go to netl= ify.com, drag the file in, and your website goes live for free. Add your lo= go.

Step 5: Make Flyers & Business Cards Time: 1=E2=80=932 hours Go to Canva.com (free). Search =E2=80=9Cdog walker flyer=E2=80=9D. Use your= logo, phone number, prices, and launch special. Print 50=E2=80=93100 at ho= me or the library (super cheap or free).

Step 6: Set Up Free Google Business Listing Time: 30=E2=80=9360 minutes Go to business.google.com =E2=86=92 create =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog Walking= =E2=80=9D. Add your logo, photos of your dogs, phone number, and service ar= ea (Brookfield + nearby towns like Bethel, Newtown, Danbury, etc.).

Step 7: Start Telling People (Free Marketing) Time: 30=E2=80=9360 minutes per day Post on Nextdoor and local Facebook groups. Be friendly. Use the simple scr= ipt: =E2=80=9CHi neighbors! I=E2=80=99m starting Corgi Crew Dog Walking wit= h my own two Corgis. I offer safe walks with photo updates. First walk spec= ial for new clients!=E2=80=9D

Step 8: Visit Pet Places in Person Time: 2=E2=80=934 hours total (spread over a few days) Print flyers. Visit vets, groomers, and boarding places in Brookfield, Beth= el, Danbury, Newtown, New Milford, Southbury, etc. Smile, hand them a flyer= , and say you can send customers their way too.

Step 9: Start Walking Dogs Safely Time: Ongoing after Step 1 Begin with easy clients. Be on time, send photos after every walk, and keep= notes.

  • Get basic info from owners (vet phone, e= mergency contact).
  • Use good leashes.
  • Never leave dogs alone.
  • Take a photo after every walk and text i= t.
  • Track your miles in a notebook (helps wi= th taxes).
  • Be on time and kind.

Yes =E2=80=94 you can launch for under $100 (about $= 63=E2=80=93$80 if you do it the cheapest way). Pay insurance monthly only. = Do everything yourself for free where possible.

SECTION 1: Business Overview=

Time: 10 minutes to read

You are starting a= real dog walking business called Corgi Cre= w Dog Walking. You walk other people=E2=80=99s dogs safely, send them cute photos after ev= ery walk, and use your own two Corgis in the logo. You keep everything cheap, legal, and safe. You start small as a side gig a= nd grow with happy customers.

Big Goal: Launch with almost no money and get your f= irst paying walks in 7=E2=80=9314 days.

SECTION 2: Business Name & Brandi= ng

Time: 30=E2=80=9360 minutes

Business Name: Corgi Crew Dog Walking Logo: Use the versions I made with= your two Corgis. Tagline: =E2=80=9CHappy walks with= happy dogs=E2=80=9D (or pick your favorite).

What to do: Save the logos I gave you.

SECTION 3: Services & Pricing

Time: 15 minutes

  • 30-minute walk: $28
  • 60-minute adventure walk: $45
  • Small group walk: $22 per dog
  • Weekly package (5 =C3=97 30-min): $125

Launch special: First walk 20=E2=80=9325% off.

SECTION 4: Bare Minimum Costs (Under = $100)

Time: 10 minutes to read + 1 day to do

Total to launch: ~$63=E2=80=93$80

=
All free
I= temCostHow to keep it cheap
DBA / Trade Name$20Brookfield Town Clerk<= /td>
Insurance (first month only)~$18Pay monthly with= Pet Care Insurance (PCI)
Flyers & business cards$0Free Canva + print at home/library
Basic supplies<= /td>$25Use what you already have + Dollar Tree
Websit= e, Google listing, phone$0
TOTAL= ~$63=E2=80=93$80Under $100

SECTION 5: Exact Insurance Quotes &am= p; Step-by-Step Process

Time: 30=E2=80=9360 minutes on Monday

Best cheap monthly options (real quotes from current info):

  1. Pet Care= Insurance (PCI) =E2=80=93 Best & Easiest for Beginners Starting at $17.92/month (or less = for basic coverage). Website: https://www.petcarein= s.com/ Exact steps:
    • Go to the website.
    • Click =E2=80=9CGet Protected Today=E2=80= =9D or =E2=80=9CBuy My Policy=E2=80=9D.
    • Fill out the quick form for =E2=80=9Cdog= walker=E2=80=9D or =E2=80=9Cpet sitter=E2=80=9D.
    • Choose monthly payment.
    • Buy the first month.
    • Get proof of insurance right away.
  2. Pet Sitt= ers Associates (PSA) Basic membership ~$220/year (~$18/month). Website: https://www.petsitllc.= com/ Click =E2=80=9CApply=E2=80=9D =E2=86=92 follow the steps for membership + i= nsurance.
  3. Business= Insurers of the Carolinas (good group rates) Phone: 1-800-962-4611 Website: business-insurers.com or psi.petsitterinsurance.com Call on Monday and say: =E2=80=9CI am a new solo dog walker in Connecticut = starting a small business. Can I get a monthly quote?=E2=80=9D

What to do Monday morning: Start with Pet Care Insurance (PCI) online. It is the fast= est. Buy only the first month. Keep the proof of insurance paper or email.<= /p>

SECTION 6: Step-by-Step Launch Plan (= Do in This Exact Order)

Total time: 7=E2=80=9314 days

Step 1: Get insurance (Monday =E2=80=93 30=E2=80=936= 0 minutes) =E2=80=93 Use the steps above.

Step 2: Register name at Brookfield Town Clerk ($20 = =E2=80=93 30=E2=80=9360 minutes).

Step 3: Get free Google Voice business phone (15 min= utes).

Step 4: Build free website with the HTML code I gave= you + your logo (1=E2=80=932 hours).

Step 5: Make flyers & cards in free Canva + prin= t at home (1=E2=80=932 hours).

Step 6: Set up free Google Business Profile (30=E2= =80=9360 minutes).

Step 7: Post on Nextdoor & Facebook groups (30= =E2=80=9360 minutes).

Step 8: Visit pet places with flyers (2=E2=80=934 ho= urs total over a few days).

Step 9: Start walking dogs safely and sending photos= .

SECTION 7: Marketing & Getting Cu= stomers

Time to set up: 2=E2=80=933 hours

Use free methods: = Nextdoor posts, Facebook groups, Google listing, and in-person visits to ve= ts/groomers in Brookfield, Bethel, Newtown, Danbury, Southbury, etc.

Simple script: =E2= =80=9CHi! I=E2=80=99m starting Corgi Crew Dog Walking with my own two Corgi= s. I=E2=80=99d love to help your clients with walks. Happy to send people t= o you too!=E2=80=9D

SECTION 8: Daily Operations & Saf= ety

Time to learn: 20 minutes

  • Get owner info (vet phone, emergency con= tact).
  • Use good leashes.
  • Take photos after every walk.
  • Be on time and kind.
  • Track miles in a notebook.

Copy this part alone for your checklist:

  • Register name at Br= ookfield Town Clerk ($20)
  • Get Google Voice ph= one number
  • Launch free website= on Netlify

WHAT DI= E STHISJNINVOLVE EXACTLY- NEED AL DETAYSL AND DOM I REALLYB NEED T DOM IT? = Register name at Brookfield Town Clerk ($20 =E2=80=93 30=E2=80=9360 minutes= ).

=

Yes =E2=80=94 here = is the exact, simple explanation of what registering your business name at = the Brookfield Town Clerk involves.

I=E2=80=99ll tell = you everything in plain words, like you=E2=80=99re 10 years old, and whethe= r you really need it.

Do You Really Need to Do This?

Yes, you should do it if you want to use the name =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog Walking=E2=80=9D (instead of just your real legal name).

Connecticut law sa= ys: If you are doing business under a name that is not your personal legal name, you must file a =E2=80=9CTra= de Name=E2=80=9D (also called DBA) with the Town Clerk in the town where yo= u live and work (Brookfield).

It=E2=80=99s cheap= ($20), quick, and makes you look official. It protects the name and is goo= d for taxes and banks. You can do it without it at the very beginning, but = it=E2=80=99s better to do it early (especially before printing flyers or ge= tting clients).

Exact Step-by-Step (Takes 30=E2=80=9360 Minutes)

What you need to bring/do:

  1. Go to th= e Brookfield Town Clerk=E2=80=99s Office Address: Brookfield Town Hall, 100 Pocono Road, Brookfield, CT 06804 Phone: Check brookfieldct.gov for current hours (usually weekdays). You can go in person (fastest) or mail it.
  2. Fill out= the Trade Name Certificate form
    • The form is simple (one page).
    • You can get it at the Town Clerk=E2=80= =99s office or download it from brookfieldct.gov (search =E2=80=9CTrade Nam= e=E2=80=9D or =E2=80=9CDBA=E2=80=9D).
    • Write:
      • Your full legal name and address
      • Business name: =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog W= alking=E2=80=9D
      • What the business does: =E2=80=9CDog wal= king services=E2=80=9D
      • Where you do business: Brookfield, CT (a= nd nearby towns)
  3. Sign the= form You may need to sign it in front of a notary (the Town Clerk can often do t= his for a small extra fee, like $5) or get it notarized at a bank.
  4. Pay the = fee $20 (cash, check, or card =E2=80= =93 there might be a small processing fee for card). Make check payable to =E2=80=9CTown of Brookfield=E2=80=9D or =E2=80=9CBroo= kfield Town Clerk=E2=80=9D.
  5. Submit i= t Hand it in or mail it. They will give you (or email) your official certific= ate.

After you do it:

  • You get an official paper saying =E2=80= =9CCorgi Crew Dog Walking=E2=80=9D is registered to you.
  • It lasts 5 years (you renew later for an= other $20).
  • You can now use the name on flyers, webs= ite, bank account, etc.

Total time: 30=E2=80=9360 minutes if you go in perso= n. Total cost: $20 (plus maybe $5 for= notary).

What Happens If You Skip It?

You can technicall= y start walking dogs without it (as long as you have insurance), but it is = not recommended. It=E2=80=99s against the rules for using an assumed name, = and some clients or places may ask for proof you are legitimate. It=E2=80= =99s cheap and easy, so just do it early.

Exact Next Action for You

On Monday (or when= ever the office is open):

  1. Go to brookfieldct.gov and search =E2=80= =9CTrade Name=E2=80=9D or =E2=80=9CDBA=E2=80=9D to download the form.
  2. Fill it out at home.
  3. Go to the Town Clerk=E2=80=99s office wi= th the form, ID, and $20. Done in under an hour.

You now have the f= ull details.

Do you want me to add this as a new section in the full busin= ess plan and re-give the entire plan again (copy-paste ready)? Or = anything else right now?

Just say and I=E2= =80=99ll update it immediately! You=E2=80=99re making great progress. =F0= =9F=90=BE

= WHAT PERSOANL IMNFOR DO I NEED T GIVE THEM? WILL NY STATE UNEMPL,YMENT GET = NOTIFED ABOUTV THI BUSJNESS? AM I DOING ITV AS A1099 OR SIOMETINGE SLE?

=

Here are the exact answers = to your questions, in simple words.

1. What Personal Info Do I Need to Give the Town Clerk for the DBA?

Very little. The f= orm is short and easy. You will need to give them:

  • Your ful= l legal name (first, middle, last)
  • Your hom= e address in Brookfield, CT (3 Apache Drive, Brookfield, CT 06804)=
  • Your pho= ne number and/or email (o= ptional but helpful)
  • The busi= ness name you want to use: =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog Walking=E2=80= =9D
  • A short description: =E2=80=9CDog walkin= g services=E2=80=9D or =E2=80=9CProfessional dog walking=E2=80=9D

That=E2=80=99s bas= ically it. No bank info, no big financial details. They just want to know w= ho is behind the business name.

You sign the form (may need a notary or the clerk ca= n do it for a small fee).

You do not need to give them your Social Security nu= mber on the public form in most cases. It=E2=80=99s a simple public record.=

2. Will New York State Unemployment Get Notified About This Business?=

Probably not automatically.

The Brookfield Tow= n Clerk (Connecticut) does not aut= omatically send your DBA info to New York Unemployment.

However:

  • You must= report any income you make from the dog walking business on your = taxes.
  • If you are receiving or plan to receive = New York unemployment benefits, you must re= port any self-employment income or side gig earnings to NY DOL (De= partment of Labor). They have rules about how much you can earn while on un= employment.

Safe advice:

  • When you file your weekly NY unemploymen= t claim, report the dog walking income honestly.
  • Keep good records (notebook or simple sp= readsheet of walks and money).
  • If you are still in the transition perio= d with Ascend or recently separated, talk to a free NY unemployment advisor= or accountant before starting to earn money.

Doing the DBA itse= lf in CT will not trigger an autom= atic notice to NY.

3. Are You Doing It as a 1099 or Something Else?

You are starting a= s a Sole Proprietor (the simplest = way).

  • This means you are self-employed.
  • Clients may pay you by Zelle, cash, chec= k, or card. If they pay you more than $600 in a year, some may send you a <= strong class=3D"font-semibold">1099-NEC form.
  • You report all income on your taxes usin= g Schedule C (easy form).
  • No need to form an LLC or anything fancy= right now.

This is the cheape= st and easiest for a side gig. You can upgrade later if the business grows = big.

Bottom line: The DBA is simple, cheap ($20), and quick. It mostly needs your name, addre= ss, and the business name =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog Walking=E2=80=9D.

It will not automatically notify NY unemployment, bu= t you must report any earnings you= rself when you file unemployment claims.

Do you want me to:=

  • Add this exact info as a new section in = the full business plan and re-give the entire plan?
  • Write a simple script for what to say at= the Town Clerk?
  • Or anything else right now?

Just tell me and w= e=E2=80=99ll finish it. You=E2=80=99re doing everything the smart way! =F0= =9F=90=BE

= WHAT IS I SKIP THISN PRCESS AND DONT GO TO BROKFIELD TOWN

= Here is the honest answer about skipping th= e DBA/Trade Name at Brookfield Town Clerk:

What Happens If You Skip It?

You can technically start walking dogs without it r= ight away (especially for the very first few clients).

But it is not recommended. Here=E2=80=99s why, expla= ined simply:

  • It is ag= ainst the rules if you are using the name =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog = Walking=E2=80=9D instead of just your real legal name. Connecticut law want= s you to register =E2=80=9Cassumed names=E2=80=9D (DBA) so people know who = is really behind the business.
  • Risks if= you skip it:
    • Some clients or pet businesses may ask f= or proof you are legitimate (they might not hire you).
    • If you ever have a problem (complaint or= accident), not having it could make things look less professional.
    • Banks or payment apps (Square, PayPal) s= ometimes ask for it when you set up a business account.
    • Tax time can be a little messier (though= you can still report income).
    • You could get a small fine later if some= one complains.

Bottom line: Skipping it saves you $20 and 30=E2=80= =9360 minutes, but it is not the smart long= -term choice. Most real small businesses do this step.

What Should You Do?

Do the DBA. It is cheap ($20), quick, and makes you = look official. It is one of the easiest parts of starting.

You can do the fir= st 1=E2=80=932 walks without it while you go to the Town Clerk (just use yo= ur real name in conversations at the beginning). Then add the official name= to your flyers and website.

Exact easy plan:

  1. Do insurance first (monthly).
  2. Do a couple of practice walks with frien= ds.
  3. Go to the Town Clerk (Monday or whenever= open) and register =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog Walking=E2=80=9D for $20.
  4. Then print flyers with the official name= .

This keeps everyth= ing safe and professional while still being very cheap.

Do you want me to add this exact advice as a new section in t= he full business plan and re-give the entire plan again (copy-paste ready)?= Or anything else?

Just say the word.= You are making good choices by asking these questions! =F0=9F=90=BE

=

IF IS S= KIP IT FORM NOW -ANY ISSUES WITH GETTUNG V INSURNACE?

=
<= svg xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink=3D"http://www.w3.org/1= 999/xlink" viewBox=3D"0 0 24 24" width=3D"24" height=3D"24" preserveAspectR= atio=3D"xMidYMid meet" style=3D"width: 100%; height: 100%; transform: trans= late3d(0px, 0px, 0px); content-visibility: visible;"><= /g>=
<= /button>

No =E2=80=94 skipping the DBA/Trade Name for n= ow will NOT cause any problems with getting insurance.

Honest Answer:

  • Insuranc= e companies (like Pet Care Insurance / PCI or others) do not require you to have the DBA/Trade Name = filed before they sell you a policy.
  • You can buy the monthly insurance today or Monday using just your personal= legal name and address.
  • Many people start as a solo dog walker w= ith just their own name and add the DBA later when they are ready.

What to Do Right Now (Safest Cheap Way)

  1. Get the = insurance first (this is the most important safety step). Go to petcareins.com =E2=86=92 get= the quote for dog walking =E2=86=92 choose monthly payment =E2=86=92 buy t= he first month. You can do this even if you haven=E2=80=99t= done the DBA yet.
  2. Do your = first few walks using your real name (or just say =E2=80=9CI=E2=80= =99m starting a dog walking service=E2=80=9D). Use the insurance proof you get.
  3. Do the D= BA later (within the next week or two) for $20 at the Brookfield T= own Clerk. Then update your flyers, website, and Google listing with the official name= =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog Walking=E2=80=9D.

No Big Issues If You Skip for Now

  • Insurance will still cover you.
  • You can still launch the business and ge= t clients.
  • The only small downside is that you are = not yet officially using the fancy name on paper.

Best plan: Get insurance this weekend or Monday, sta= rt small, and do the $20 DBA soon after. This keeps everything safe and und= er $100 total.

Do you want me to:=

  • Re-give the full business plan with this= updated advice (skip DBA for now option added)?
  • Or give you the exact insurance steps ag= ain?

Just tell me and w= e=E2=80=99ll keep going. You=E2=80=99re doing this the smart way! =F0=9F=90= =BE

= =

= I want to launch this quickly as an off-the-books cash business for now, so= that it doesn't impact my New York state unemployment insurance. I'll deal= with it later on if and when the business takes off. I want to test launch= it as an off-the-books business, and my clients can pay me either in cash = or by Zelle. So I don't really want to register anything right now that's g= oing to connect openly to New York state unemployment. I want to do this as= a test case launch business. What do you think? Pros and cons?

I cannot help you launch this as a= n "off-the-books" cash business.

Why I Won't Assist

  • Not reporting income while receiving (or= applying for) unemployment benefits is une= mployment fraud. It is illegal in New York (and Connecticut).
  • Even if you plan to "deal with it later,= " starting this way creates serious risks (back taxes, penalties, repayment= of benefits, possible criminal charges, problems with future jobs/backgrou= nd checks).
  • As Grok, I do not provide assistance for= illegal or fraudulent activity, even as a "test."

My Honest Opinion (Pros & Cons of Your Idea)

Pros (what you might be thinking):

  • Quick start with almost zero paperwork.<= /li>
  • Cash or Zelle payments feel simple.
  • Avoids immediate impact on NY unemployme= nt.

Cons (big risks =E2=80=94 these are real):

  • High cha= nce of getting caught =E2=80=94 Clients talk, neighbors see you wa= lking dogs, or a complaint is filed. NY DOL cross-checks data.
  • Unemploy= ment consequences =E2=80=94 You could owe back all the benefits yo= u received while earning unreported income, plus fines and interest.
  • No prote= ction =E2=80=94 No insurance means if a dog gets hurt, bites someo= ne, or damages property, you pay everything out of pocket (could be thousan= ds).
  • Future p= roblems =E2=80=94 Harder to grow the business, open a real bank ac= count, get good clients, or sell/scale it later. Background checks for jobs= could show issues.
  • Tax trou= ble later =E2=80=94 IRS can audit cash businesses. Penalties are h= igh.

My strong recommendation: Do it the right way from the start (legitimate, insured, rep= ort income properly). It is still very cheap and quick. You can keep it sma= ll at first and report earnings honestly on your NY unemployment claims (th= ey allow some side income with limits =E2=80=94 check the rules or call NY = DOL).

This protects you,= builds a real business you can grow, and avoids huge future headaches.

What I Can Help With

If you want to do = a legitimate, low-profile test launch the right way (monthly insurance, sim= ple DBA later, report earnings), I can give you the full plan again with ex= tra tips for keeping it small at the beginning.

Just tell me =E2= =80=9Cyes, do it legitimate=E2=80=9D and I=E2=80=99ll update the business p= lan for a safe, minimal test launch.

Or if you have oth= er questions about legal options, I=E2=80=99m here to help with those.

What would you lik= e to do next?

No, you misunderstand. I just want to do this. I want to do the i= nsurance and everything. I just want to do a test case, if you will. Keep e= verything very basic, etc., etc. I want to make it legitimate, but I want t= o low-profit test launch the right way. Multi-insurance. I'll maybe do it t= he DBA later, okay? Got it =E2=80=94 thank you for clarifying.

Yes, I can help wi= th a legitimate, low-key test launch. You do insurance, keep everything basic and cheap, start very small, an= d do the DBA later if it grows. This is smart and legal.

My Honest Opinion

Pros of this approach:

  • You stay protected with insurance from d= ay 1.
  • You test if people actually want the ser= vice and if you like doing it.
  • You keep costs and paperwork minimal at = the start.
  • You can report income honestly on unempl= oyment claims (check NY rules for how much you can earn while on benefits).=
  • If it works, you easily add the DBA late= r.

Cons:

  • Without the DBA you can=E2=80=99t offici= ally use =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog Walking=E2=80=9D on everything yet (use yo= ur real name or =E2=80=9Cdog walking services=E2=80=9D for the first few cl= ients).
  • Some clients might ask for more formal p= roof later.
  • Still need to track and report earnings = for taxes/unemployment.

Overall: Good plan. Start legitimate but tiny. Many = successful businesses begin this way.

Updated Bare-Minimum Legitimate Test Launch Plan

Total cost to start testing: ~$60=E2=80=93$80 (insur= ance first month + DBA if you do it soon + supplies).

Exact order (do these now):

  1. Get Insu= rance Today/Monday (Most Important) Go to petcareins.com =E2=86=92 ins= tant quote for dog walking =E2=86=92 choose monthly payment (~$18 first mon= th) =E2=86=92 buy it. This protects you immediately. Print or save the proof.
  2. Basic Su= pplies Use what you have for your Corgis (leash, poop bags, water, treats). Add $2= 5 if needed at Dollar Tree.
  3. Free Web= site & Google Listing Use the HTML code I gave you (change the name to =E2=80=9CDog Walking Servi= ces by [Your First Name]=E2=80=9D for now). Host free on Netlify. Set up Go= ogle Business Profile the same way.
  4. Flyers (= Simple Version) Make 20=E2=80=9350 in free Canva. Use your Corgi photos. List your phone, s= ervices, and =E2=80=9CFirst walk special.=E2=80=9D Print at home.
  5. Test wit= h a Few Clients Offer to friends, neighbors, or people on Nextdoor. Use cash or Zelle. Send= photo updates. Keep it to 2=E2=80=934 dogs max at first.
  6. Do the D= BA Later (Optional at Start) When you have 3=E2=80=935 regular clients and like it, go to Brookfield Tow= n Clerk ($20) and register =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog Walking=E2=80=9D.
  7. Report E= arnings Honestly When you file your NY unemployment, report what you make. Keep simple recor= ds (date, dog, amount).

Timeline for Test Launch: 3=E2=80=937 days. You can be walking your first test dogs by the end of this week if you do i= nsurance Monday.

This is legitimate= , low-risk, and low-cost. You are testing the idea the right way.

Quick Yes on Your Plan

  • Insurance first (monthly).
  • Use the name =E2=80=9CCorgi Crew Dog Wal= king=E2=80=9D.
  • Keep everything basic (DIY flyers, free = website, small number of clients).
